headline: Parliamentary committee system has zero subpoena power
context: None of Malaysia's 14 parliamentary select committees possess subpoena power, relying entirely on voluntary cooperation from government witnesses and document providers.

cards.big: 14 parliamentary committees — zero subpoena power to compel witnesses
cards.sub: Government officials can simply decline to appear before oversight committees

cards.big: Houses of Parliament (Privileges and Powers) Act 1952 has no subpoena provision
cards.sub: 70-year-old legislation predates modern parliamentary accountability standards

cards.big: PAC investigation into LTAT in 2025 stalled when 3 key witnesses refused to appear
cards.sub: Billion-ringgit scandals go uninvestigated because committees cannot compel attendance

cards.big: India's parliamentary committees can summon any citizen under threat of contempt
cards.sub: Even within Asia, Malaysia's committee powers are unusually weak

cards.big: Oversight without enforcement power is just a polite request that can be politely refused
Should select committees receive subpoena power through constitutional amendment?
